-- 1 of 46 --

Welcome to Advanced Influencer Marketing!
I’m Gretta van Riel and I’ve co-founded four multi-
million dollar ecommerce stores. One of my brands,
The 5th Watches, achieved $1 million dollars in
sales in a single day. I’ve also launched Influencer
Marketing platform called Hey Influencers which has
helped link thousands of social media influencers to
brands.
I’ve been in the ecommerce game since 2012 and
since then the biggest key to success across all
of my brands has been influencer marketing. So in
this module I’ve wrapped up the biggest lessons
I’ve learned when trying to scale my influencer
marketing strategies as my businesses have grown.
Specifically, I’ll teach you my proven methods to
boost conversions (above all else), increase your
following, and drive more traffic to your website.
LESSON:
MODULE INTRO

-- 4 of 46 --

In this lesson, I’m going to teach you how to find
influencers to work with that can leverage an
audience to influence their buying decisions.
This is the most important part in the influencer
marketing process because if you choose the wrong
influencer, you’d be spending time, money and
product without getting conversions in return. Lucky
for you, I’ve distilled this process down to a science.
Influencer + Reach = Brand Exposure
Influencer + Influence = Conversions
Influencers - Influence = Content Creator
The Four Types of Influencers
• 	Nano-Influencers: 1,000-10,000 followers
• 	Micro-Influencer : 10,000-50,000 followers
• 	Macro-Influencers: 500,000+ followers
• 	Mega-Influencers: 1 million + followers
The 3 R’s
Reach x Relevance x Relationships = Influence
Tips for Reach
• 	Look at reach not as a static figure but as a
moving average
• 	To determine reach focus on month-on-month
follower growth
Tips for Relevancy
• 	Understand who your target audience or ideal
customer or customer persona is
• 	Use influencers that have credibility and
authority on the subject matter
LESSON:
IDENTIFYING THE
INFLUENTIAL
MODULE:
ADVANCED INFLUENCER
MARKETING
ACTION ITEMS
❏ 	List influencers that would make sense for your
brand for each of the 3 R’s.
❏ 	Use the Social Blade tool linked in the
Resources to find influencers in your niche that
are growing fast.
“Not all self-proclaimed influencers have influence.”
Tips for Relationship
• 	Use engagement as one of the most accurate
indicators of Relationship
• 	Find influencers with almost a cult-like following
• 	Look for influencers who are popular across
multiple platforms

-- 8 of 46 --

In this lesson, I’m going to teach you the exact
process of the customer journey from brand
discovery (through an influencer) to purchase. This
is called the Influencer Funnel. With this information,
you’ll be able to build and optimize your own funnel
leading your customers directly to purchase.
LESSON:
INFLUENCER FUNNELS
MODULE:
ADVANCED INFLUENCER
MARKETING
ACTION ITEMS
❏ 	Map out your 4-stage influencer funnel in the
workbook.
“Your instagram account acts like the initial landing page
for your influencer marketing activities.”
Tips for Profile Conversion
• 	Feed
• Content should have a similar look, feel, and
aesthetic to your influencers
• 	Bio
• Who you are/what you’re selling
• Unique Value Proposition
• Call-to-Action
• CTA Examples
• 	Link in Bio
• Waitlist landing page
• Content unlock
• Best-sellers
• Shoppable feed

-- 12 of 46 --

In the next two lessons we’ll go through nano and
micro influencer campaigns to run at scale. We’ll
start off with the Product-for-Content campaign.
This is a type of collaboration in which the influencer
submits content to the brand rather than posting it
themselves.
When you’re working with influencers, aim for at least
a 50% completion. However, this campaign is key
because it leads to a higher campaign completion
rate (90%) than the Product-for-Post campaign we’ll
discuss in the next lesson (50%).
LESSON:
PRODUCT-FOR-CONTENT
CAMPAIGN
MODULE:
ADVANCED INFLUENCER
MARKETING
ACTION ITEMS
❏ 	Use the influencer brief template provided
to create one for your own Product-for-Post
Campaign.
“Your instagram account acts like the initial landing page
for your influencer marketing activities.”
Working with Micro/Nano Influencers
Pros
• 	Work for free product
• 	Cheaper
Cons
• 	Lower campaign completion rates
• 	No binding contract
• 	Time-consuming
Breakeven Factors
• 	Management time
• 	Product cost
• 	Shipping
How to Increase Campaign Completion
• 	Building the relationship
• 	Following up
• 	Small payment or voucher
• 	Offer product once they’ve posted
COURSE WORKBOOK
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 46 --

DIY Influencer Brief
Use the example provided to create your own influencer brief for a Product-for-Post campaign. Once you’ve completed
the brief, reach out to an influencer and follow up by sending them the brief.
Example: Calming Blankets
We are looking for our [Product] to be trialled by influencers on [Platform] via a [Photo/Video]. You will describe the
benefits of [Product Type]. Furthermore mention how it has helped you with [Benefit]. We can create you a unique code
to allow us to do future collaborations. Any content created will need to be sent to us as we may decide to repost.
In terms of the deliverables of this campaign, we’re asking for a [Photo/Video Spec and [Number of edited photos/
videos] + [Number of non-edited photos] as if you were going to post them to [Platform] (but no need to post to social)
in return for a [Product] (valued at [Product Price]). We look forward to working with you!
Brief:

-- 16 of 46 --

In this lesson, we’ll talk about a second type of
scalable micro-influencer collaboration: the Product-
for-Post Campaign. LIke the previous one, it’s pretty
much self explanatory. Here, the brand would offer
their product in return for an influencer post.
The reason why these have much lower completion
rates than the previous campaign is because
influencers don’t want to overcrowd their feed with
branded posts. However, this campaign is very
important because you’ll be more closely leveraging
the influencer’s relationship with their audience. So
they tend to be more effective.

Influencer/s:
Deliverables:
Offer:
CTA:
Date:

-- 20 of 46 --

As we’ve previously learned, you would need to work
with dozens if not hundreds of micro-influencers to
get the same reach as one macro influencer. That’s
why in the next two lessons, you’ll learn how to
manage working with hundreds of micro-influencers.
This is important because working with micro-
influencers at scale costs less and creates a
compounding effect for your audience as if everyone
has your product. So your goal here will be to target
50-100 send-outs a month for the next three months.
Working with Micro-Influencers at Scale
• 	Step 1: Create a new instagram account
• 	Step 2: Follow 100-1000 micro influencers from
the new account
• 	Step 3: Hire a Virtual Assistant (VA) to reach out
to each influencer
• 	Step 4: Engage with the influencer’s profile
• 	Step 5: Send a follow-up message
LESSON:
SCALING MICRO-
INFLUENCERS

We’ve just covered one method for influencer
outreach: the Instagram direct message/email
message. The other way we reach out to our
influencers is through a platform called Woodpecker.
In this lesson, you’ll learn about Woodpecker as well
as my go-to platform to track the success of my
influencer campaigns: PeopleMap.

OUTREACH

-- 27 of 46 --

While macro-influencers can be the most effective,
they’re also the most expensive. In this lesson, I’ll
teach you how to leverage the power of macro-
influencer campaigns to maximize every dollar spent.
First we’ll cover how to attribute ROI, other methods
to reduce cost, followed by my top macro-influencer
content hack and the macro-influencer campaigns
that have been proven to work best.
Personalized Codes (for ROI Attribution)
❏ 	Discounts
❏ 	Free Shipping
❏ 	Store Vouchers
❏ 	Flash Sale
❏ 	Free Gift with Purchase
Tips for Cost Reduction
❏ 	Have a compelling product that influencers will
want
❏ 	Maintain good personal and professional
relationships
❏ 	Build a large enough social following
❏ 	Lock in long-term engagements
❏ 	Find YouTube influencers on Instagram
LESSON:
MACRO-INFLUENCER
COLLABORATIONS
MODULE:
ADVANCED INFLUENCER
MARKETING
ACTION ITEMS
❏ 	Find a macro influencer to work with.
❏ 	Implement some of the strategies in this lesson.
Macro Influencer Campaigns
❏ 	Influencer Cluster Campaign
❏ 	Tag-to-Win Giveaway
Tips for a Giveaway
❏ 	Include a strong, clear CTA
❏ 	Limit the timespan
❏ 	Don’t ask followers to do too much
❏ 	Make it simple
Steps for a Giveaway
❏ 	Choose the right influencer
❏ 	Craft a compelling description
❏ 	Add an extra step to hack engagement

-- 31 of 46 --

In this lesson, you’ll learn how to run an Influencer Ad
Takeover campaign on Facebook. This is a campaign
in which an influencer gives a brand advertiser
access to their content to run dark posts or ads that
don’t appear on the poster’s newsfeed. To help you
understand the power of the ad takeover, we’ll be
comparing two scenarios: a regular pay-per-post
campaign and an influencer ad takeover campaign.
Influencer Marketing + Paid Advertising =
Influencer Ad Takeover
Tips for an Influencer Ad Takeover
• 	Make sure you have the right to repurpose
content
• 	Request audience insights to help determine
influencer/brand fit
LESSON:

YouTubers tend to have a more dedicated brand
following than most Instagram influencers. In this
lesson, I’ll teach you how to leverage the power of
YouTube influencers to increase conversions for your
brand.
Types of YouTube Videos
❏ 	Reviews
❏ 	Most Recommended
❏ 	How-To’s
❏ 	Tutorials
❏ 	Joint Giveaways
❏ 	Unboxing videos
YouTube Benefits
• 	Evergreen Content
• 	SEO
• 	Influencer Conversion Rate
• 	UTM Tracking
Reducing Cost
• 	Pay influencer via an affiliate code
• 	Don’t take the influencer’s initial price
• 	Negotiate long-term agreements
• 	Get a cross-platform deal
Influencer Discovery
• 	Perform a keyword search for topics similar to
your product
• 	Find influencers for other brands in your
category
• 	Use the “Up Next” sidebar to find related videos
• 	Search featured lists of YouTubers in your topic
area
LESSON:

INFLUENCERS

-- 39 of 46 --

So you’ve fulfilled your end of the deal. You’ve
sent the influencer your product and created a
personalized discount code. Now it’s time for the
influencer to fulfill their end. In this lesson, I’ll teach
you tried and tested ways to follow-up with your
influencers (macro or micro) that will continue a
positive working relationship.
The “Unpaid” Follow-Up
• 	Structure your follow-up like a reminder
• 	Assume positive intent
• 	Compliment them
• 	Stay positive, upbeat and energetic
• 	Be understanding and acknowledge their effort
“Unpaid” Follow-Up Examples
• 	“Just checking in to see if you received the
product. Have you had a chance to have a play
with it yet?”
• 	“I can't wait to see what you create. We just love
your content!”
• 	“I know you're really busy, but we're really
looking forward to seeing what you create.”
• 	“Just checking in to see if there's anything else
you need from our end to continue.”
The “Paid” Follow-Up
• 	Be direct but polite
• 	Send brief reminders
• 	Issue calendar invites
• 	Be polite but persistent
• 	Leverage the power of introductions
LESSON:
MASTERING THE FOLLOW-UP

MARKETING

-- 43 of 46 --

In this lesson, I’ll teach you how to create a great,
long-lasting relationship with your influencers. You
want to create enough “good will” among your
influencers so that next time you’d need them for a
campaign, they won’t hesitate to agree to work with
you.
The Sweetener Framework
• 	Make the influencer feel INCLUDED
• Include in brand’s why
• What we do what we do and why
• 	Make the influencer feel SPECIAL
• Why you chose them over others
• Make communication and collaboration as
personalized to them as possible
• 	Make the influencer feel VIP
• Send them gifts
Gift Ideas
• 	Hand-written thank you card
• 	Product customization
• 	Influencer only products
• 	Product gift packs
• 	Flowers
• 	All-expenses paid experience
• 	Branded retreat
LESSON:
THE SWEETENER
FRAMEWORK
MODULE:
ADVANCED INFLUENCER
MARKETING
ACTION ITEMS
